# Development Progress Checklist: A股开盘核心新闻聚合研究 Skill

**Purpose**: Validate whether current development progress, completion claims, and remaining review gates are clearly documented and traceable across the feature documents.
**Created**: 2026-04-29
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the /speckit.checklist command based on feature context and progress-related documentation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all completed delivery slices explicitly identified by phase and user story so a reviewer can determine whether MVP, US2, and US3 are all included in the claimed completion state? [Completeness, Spec Implementation Progress, Tasks Phase 3-5]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the remaining work after the "T001-T044 已完成" claim documented explicitly, including human review, merge readiness, and any release gate still open? [Gap, Spec Status, Spec Implementation Progress, Tasks Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK003 Are objective evidence locations defined for each claimed validation closure rather than summarized only as “本地验证闭环已通过”? [Completeness, Plan Constitution Check, Tasks T040-T042, Remediation Log]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK004 Is the distinction between "code complete", "validation complete", and "ready for review" explicitly defined so progress terms are not overloaded? [Clarity, Spec Status, Plan Summary]
- [ ] CHK005 Are deferred post-MVP items clearly separated from current-slice completion so long-horizon reliability goals are not misread as open implementation blockers? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002, Plan Deferred After MVP, Tasks MVP Scope]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the phrase "当前状态为待人工 review" qualified with reviewer role, review scope, and exit condition? [Ambiguity, Spec Implementation Progress]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK007 Do completion statements in the spec align with the all-checked task list and phase checkpoint language without contradicting the remaining review gate? [Consistency, Spec Implementation Progress, Tasks Phase 1-6, Plan Task Plan Rationality Review]
- [ ] CHK008 Are temporary-report and final-report completion claims consistent between the staged-publication requirements and the task checkpoints? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Plan Summary, Tasks Phase 3]
- [ ] CHK009 Are progress claims about configuration-driven scope evolution consistent with both the specification requirements and the Phase 5 completion checkpoint? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Tasks Phase 5]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK010 Can a reviewer objectively determine from the documents whether SC-003 and SC-005 are satisfied, or are additional explicit pass/fail signals still missing? [Acceptance Criteria Quality, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-005, Tasks Phase 4-6]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the validation commands and pass criteria written as auditable acceptance evidence rather than implicit expectations? [Measurability, Spec §FR-019, Plan Constitution Check, Tasks T040-T042]
- [ ] CHK012 Is repository hygiene completion measurable from documented evidence rather than inferred from a checked task alone? [Measurability, Spec §FR-020, Tasks T043]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK013 Are partial-success, source-missing, and staged-publication scenarios explicitly included in the completion claim for the current progress state? [Coverage, Spec User Story 1, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-014, Tasks T019-T020]
- [ ] CHK014 Are operator handoff and manual-review scenarios included in the definition of "开发完成", not just code execution scenarios? [Coverage, Spec User Story 2, Spec §FR-010, Tasks Phase 4]

## Dependencies & Ambiguities

- [ ] CHK015 Are dependencies on local environment, example config, and cross-platform behavior clearly stated as satisfied prerequisites for the claimed status? [Dependencies & Assumptions, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018, Plan Technical Context, Tasks Phase 2 and Phase 6]
- [ ] CHK016 Is there one authoritative status source for future progress updates, or do spec, plan, tasks, and remediation documents risk drifting over time? [Conflict, Spec Status, Plan Summary, Tasks Checkpoints, Remediation Log]
